seed_papers.title: Algorithms for quantum computation: discrete logarithms and factoring
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Reference model for a Shor-level algorithm: reduction plus Fourier sampling plus classical postprocessing.

seed_papers.title: The Hidden Subgroup Problem
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Maps the HSP search space and nonabelian barriers.

seed_papers.title: The Symmetric Group Defies Strong Fourier Sampling
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Prevents the project from rediscovering a known-dead GI strategy.

seed_papers.title: Quantum computation of Fourier transforms over symmetric groups
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Makes the S_n quantum Fourier transform a solved polynomial-time primitive; a new coset-state route must identify the additional recoupling and decoding operations rather than relabel QFT as the obstacle.

seed_papers.title: Efficient Quantum Circuits for Schur and Clebsch-Gordan Transforms
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Provides efficient Schur-Weyl/Clebsch-Gordan circuits and weak irrep projection, but does not by itself supply the internal Specht-module Kronecker transform needed by symmetric-group coset-state decoding.

seed_papers.title: A remark on the quantum complexity of the Kronecker coefficients
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Places exact Kronecker multiplicity counting in #BQP using commuting projectors; counting an invariant space is weaker than coherently resolving a basis and its state-dependent transition amplitudes.

seed_papers.title: Quantum Algorithms for Representation-Theoretic Multiplicities
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Gives quantum multiplicity algorithms under dimension-ratio restrictions, but the revised paper no longer supports the original conjecture of a superpolynomial Kronecker advantage on those families.

seed_papers.title: Polynomial time classical versus quantum algorithms for representation theoretic multiplicities
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Classically resolves many restricted Kronecker and plethysm instances previously proposed as quantum speedups, sharply limiting multiplicity estimation as an independent breakthrough direction.

seed_papers.title: High-dimensional quantum Schur transforms
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Corrects a multiplicity-space step that had been treated as a classical permutation and makes the required quantum isometry/F-moves explicit, a direct warning against hand-waving recoupling registers.

seed_papers.title: Random dilation superchannel
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Defines the internal S_n Kronecker transform as a basis-change primitive in a circuit identity, but does not turn that definition into the hidden-involution recoupling and decoding theorem needed here.

seed_papers.title: Quantum Fourier sampling, Code Equivalence, and the quantum security of the McEliece and Sidelnikov cryptosystems
seed_papers.why_it_matters: States the search code-equivalence HSP construction and warns that HSP measurement hardness is irrelevant when support splitting or other classical invariants solve the code family.

seed_papers.title: Permutation Code Equivalence is not Harder than Graph Isomorphism when Hulls are Trivial
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Gives an exact polynomial reduction from trivial-hull permutation code equivalence to weighted graph isomorphism and a shortening upper bound parameterized by hull dimension, preventing random planted codes from being treated as independent nonabelian hardness without a hull audit.

seed_papers.title: A subexponential-time quantum algorithm for the dihedral hidden subgroup problem
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Core algorithmic template for hidden shift and phase-state combining.

seed_papers.title: Quantum Computation and Lattice Problems
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Connects efficient dihedral HSP progress to lattice breakthroughs.

seed_papers.title: The Multivariate Hidden Number Problem
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Separates uniform random-multiplier hidden-number access from chosen-multiplier Fourier methods and warns that the latter do not automatically solve the uniform distribution model.

seed_papers.title: Finding Significant Fourier Coefficients: Clarifications, Simplifications, Applications and Limitations
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Provides a primary-source guide to significant-Fourier-coefficient algorithms and their access limitations, preventing chosen-query tools from being imported into random-label DCP decoding without proof.

seed_papers.title: Sparse Fourier Transform in Any Constant Dimension with Nearly-Optimal Sample Complexity in Sublinear Time
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Provides a polylogarithmic sparse-spectrum localization benchmark, but its HashToBins measurements use algorithm-selected shifted and correlated sample locations that must not be conflated with iid DCP labels.

seed_papers.title: Quantum algorithms to solve the hidden shift problem for quadratics and for functions of large Gowers norm
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Shows that higher-order Fourier structure is an algorithmic handle, not just a statistic.

seed_papers.title: Efficient Quantum Algorithms for Shifted Quadratic Character Problems
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Provides the quantum Fourier template for shifted quadratic characters over finite fields.

seed_papers.title: Solving Shift Problems and Hidden Coset Problem Using the Fourier Transform
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Generalizes shifted multiplicative-character recovery through finite-field Fourier deconvolution.

seed_papers.title: On the Hidden Shifted Power Problem
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Supplies classical query/time upper bounds that prevent shifted-character query gaps from being overstated.

seed_papers.title: Polynomial time key-recovery attack on high rate random alternant codes
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Shows that shortening, componentwise products, conductors, and algebraic recovery can destroy apparent alternant-code hardness.

seed_papers.title: On the dimension and structure of the square of the dual of a Goppa code
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Relates the dual Goppa square-code dimension to quadratic relations and supplies rigorous parameter-dependent bounds for classical distinguishers.

seed_papers.title: The syzygy distinguisher
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Generalizes square-code tests to higher linear-strand Betti invariants of shortened dual codes and extends the classical distinguishing regime.

seed_papers.title: Cryptanalysis of public-key cryptosystems that use subcodes of algebraic geometry codes
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Introduces t-closure-based polynomial attacks that can reconstruct ambient algebraic-geometry codes from subcodes, closing a major gap left by dimension-only Schur tests.

seed_papers.title: A geometric invariant of linear rank-metric codes
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Extends Schur-power dimension-sequence thinking to geometric invariants that distinguish Gabidulin structure.

seed_papers.title: Polynomial-Time Quantum Algorithms for Pell's Equation and the Principal Ideal Problem
seed_papers.why_it_matters: A non-Shor number-theoretic breakthrough template based on hidden periodic structure.

seed_papers.title: Span programs and quantum query complexity: The general adversary bound is nearly tight for every boolean function
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Turns lower-bound machinery into algorithm synthesis machinery for query problems.

seed_papers.title: Quantum singular value transformation and beyond
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Modern unifying framework for many quantum algorithms; useful for abstraction mining.

seed_papers.title: A CS guide to the quantum singular value transformation
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Makes QSVT components easier to represent as reusable program abstractions.

seed_papers.title: Discovering Quantum Circuit Components with Program Synthesis
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Useful for subroutine discovery, but must be subordinated to scalable math structure.

seed_papers.title: Automated Quantum Algorithm Synthesis
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Evidence that example-driven synthesis can rediscover structure; also a warning about rediscovery-only systems.

seed_papers.title: Quantum Algorithms for Gowers Norm Estimation, Polynomial Structure Testing, and Counting Arithmetic Progressions
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Recent signal that higher-order harmonic analysis is an active quantum algorithms interface.

seed_papers.title: Randomized Quantum Singular Value Transformation
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Suggests a frontier around replacing costly block encodings with randomized primitives.

seed_papers.title: Quantum state isomorphism problems for groups
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Recent nearby problem class linking group actions, quantum states, and hidden-shift style structure.

seed_papers.title: Automated near-term quantum algorithm discovery for molecular ground states
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Shows useful automation patterns, but targets heuristic near-term chemistry rather than Shor-level complexity.

seed_papers.title: Proving Quantum Programs Correct
seed_papers.why_it_matters: Points toward proof-checking candidate algorithms rather than trusting generated explanations.
